title: Impeaching John Deacon Bates, a judge of the United States District Court for the District of Columbia, for high crimes and misdemeanors.
plain_summary: This bill is about removing a federal judge named John Deacon Bates from his job. Congress wants to impeach him because he ordered government agencies to put LGBTQI+ content back on their websites after it had been taken down. Congress thinks his decision was wrong and shows he cannot be trusted to be a fair judge. If this bill passes, Judge Bates would lose his job as a federal judge.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This is a resolution to impeach John Deacon Bates. He is a judge of the United States District Court for the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives wants to remove him from his job for high crimes and misdemeanors (serious wrongdoing by government officials).

sections.title: Article of Impeachment
sections.text: The House of Representatives accuses Judge John Deacon Bates of acting in ways that break the trust people should have in a federal judge. Judge Bates ordered three government agencies to put back certain content on their websites. These agencies are the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the Department of Health and Human Services, and the Food and Drug Administration. The content was about LGBTQI+ issues and had been removed following an executive order signed on January twentieth, twenty twenty five. Judge Bates gave a temporary restraining order to a group called Doctors for America. In his order, he said the agencies likely failed to properly explain why they removed the medical information from their websites. He also said the agencies did not think about how medical professionals had been using these web pages. The resolution claims that Judge Bates failed to consider that these web pages had information about gender affirming care. The resolution describes this care as intentional surgical or chemical castration of children. It argues that judges in American history would not have seen purposeful damage to healthy young people's bodies as a legitimate health concern. The resolution states that Congress believes promoting what it calls this grave moral evil requires immediate action. Therefore, Congress says Judge Bates has acted without intellectual honesty and basic integrity. They say he is guilty of high crimes and misdemeanors, is not fit to be a federal judge, and should be removed from office.
